chairman_synthesis.role: Synthesized by Training Agent from 4 council responses (full quorum)

consensus_areas.root_cause: All 4 models agree: The repetition loop is caused primarily by (c) CoT format too repetitive + (b) 5 epochs causing pattern memorization + (a) insufficient data diversity. The rigid '- col:val: N rows → [Row ...] ✓' pattern creates a local attractor state that the model enters and cannot escape. Claude adds mathematical proof: if P(continue pattern)=0.95 per item, P(terminating over 50 items) = 0.95^50 ≈ 7.7%. This explains why small datasets work but large ones loop — it's a PROBABILITY problem, not a knowledge problem.
consensus_areas.alpha_r_ratio_was_wrong: Claude's UNIQUE insight (other 3 missed the ratio): v2 used alpha/r = 16/64 = 0.25, meaning LoRA updates were very weak. This is the #2 most impactful fix. With proper ratio of 2.0 (alpha=64/r=32), LoRA updates land with correct scale. All 4 models agree on r=32, but Claude shows the ratio matters more than absolute rank.
consensus_areas.repetition_fix: UNANIMOUS (4/4): Do NOT use repetition_penalty or no_repeat_ngram_size. Instead use: (1) Constrained decoding / grammar-guided generation (outlines library), (2) Custom StoppingCriteria at </think>, (3) Dynamic max_new_tokens based on dataset size, (4) Temperature increase (Claude: 0.3, others: 0.05-0.3) + top_k=40-50. Claude adds: temp=0.1 makes loops WORSE (too deterministic, locks into attractor). Recommend temp=0.3.
consensus_areas.training_data: UNANIMOUS (4/4): Make CoT shorter — remove evidence_rows from <think> section, only put counts. Add RESULT SUMMARY termination signal. Expand to 800-1200 examples with curriculum learning (simple→medium→complex). Claude provides concrete compact format: 'age: young=4(R1,R2,R4,R7)✓ old=3(R3,R5,R8)✓' — one LINE per column, not per value.
consensus_areas.dpo: UNANIMOUS (4/4): YES, include DPO after SFT. Use 1 epoch, low LR. Chosen=concise+correct, Rejected=repetitive loops from v2 failures. Beta differs: Claude=0.05, others=0.1. Claude warns DPO cannot fix broken SFT — must fix SFT FIRST.
consensus_areas.grpo: UNANIMOUS (4/4): SKIP GRPO for v3. Too unstable without solid base. GRPO requires diverse rollouts; if model loops, all rollouts get penalized equally → zero-variance gradient → model collapses to empty outputs (as observed in v1).
consensus_areas.lora_rank: CONSENSUS (4/4): Reduce from r=64 to r=32. High rank overfits formatting template.
consensus_areas.lora_alpha: CONSENSUS: alpha=64 for ratio=2.0. Gemini: 2x rank, DeepSeek: alpha=64, Grok: alpha=32, Claude: alpha=64. Median: alpha=64.
consensus_areas.learning_rate: Reduce from 2e-4. Gemini: 5e-5, DeepSeek: 1.5e-4, Grok: 1e-4, Claude: 1e-4. Median: 1e-4.
consensus_areas.epochs: CONSENSUS (4/4): 2-3 epochs (not 5). With early stopping. Go with 3.
consensus_areas.seq_length: SPLIT: 3 say reduce to 2048. Claude says INCREASE to 6000 (don't truncate valid large-dataset CoT). Compromise: use 2048 for training (forces concise), but 4096+ at inference.
consensus_areas.save_method: UNANIMOUS (4/4): NEVER use merged_4bit_forced. Use adapter-only loading (PeftModel.from_pretrained). If merge needed, merge in FP16 first, then quantize. Claude explains why: quantization error (±scale_factor per weight) is LARGER than LoRA deltas when alpha/r=0.25, so they get lost.
consensus_areas.data_expansion: Gemini: 1000, DeepSeek: 748, Grok: 1200, Claude: 1000. Target: ~1000 examples total.
consensus_areas.evaluation_gate: Claude's UNIQUE contribution: Add explicit pass/fail gate between SFT and DPO. Phase 2 criteria: parse_rate≥0.70, mean_F1≥0.55, loop_rate≤0.30. Do NOT proceed to DPO if SFT is broken.

disagreements.lora_rank_exact: Gemini suggests r=16-32, DeepSeek/Grok/Claude all say r=32. Go with r=32.
disagreements.dpo_beta: Claude recommends 0.05 (conservative, avoid reward hacking). Others say 0.1. Claude warns DPO can cause format degradation if SFT base is poor. Go with 0.1 but monitor closely.
disagreements.temperature: Claude says 0.3 (higher = escape attractors). Others suggest 0.05-0.1. Claude's reasoning is strong: at temp=0.1, model is near-deterministic and LOCKS into loops. Recommend 0.3 for eval, 0.1 for production if no loops.
disagreements.seq_length_train: 3 models say 2048, Claude says 6000. Compromise: train with 2048 (forces concise CoT), inference with 4096.
disagreements.lora_dropout: Gemini: not mentioned, DeepSeek: 0.1, Grok: not mentioned, Claude: 0.05. Go with 0.05.
disagreements.data_format_variance: Gemini suggests 3 CoT levels (Full/Fast/Direct-to-JSON), DeepSeek suggests adding negative examples, Grok suggests curriculum phases, Claude suggests column-grouped compact format. All compatible — do all.

final_v3_plan.overview: 2-Phase training: SFT (concise CoT) → DPO (anti-repetition). Skip GRPO.

tasks: Generate ~1000 SFT examples with CONCISE CoT (no evidence rows in <think>, only counts)
Prepare DPO pairs: chosen=concise correct, rejected=v2 repetition loop failures (500-600 pairs)

phase_1_sft.success_criteria: Val F1 > 0.60, parse rate > 80%, no repetition loops in val set

phase_2_dpo.pairs_strategy: 70% real v2 failures as rejected, 30% synthetic loop examples. Chosen=concise+correct.

inference_config.note: Claude: temp=0.3 escapes attractors; temp=0.1 locks into loops. Use two-phase if possible.

fallbacks: If SFT F1 < 0.60: reduce r=16, alpha=32, epochs=2
If loops persist: enforce grammar with outlines/lm-format-enforcer
If DPO hurts: skip DPO, focus on better SFT data
If F1 < 0.75 final: v3.1 with 2000 examples + constrained decoding
Ultimate: two-stage generation (CoT model → JSON parser model)

immediate_actions_for_v2_adapter: Implement </think> StoppingCriteria for current v2 adapter
Test with temperature=0.3 (Claude: 0.1 makes loops WORSE), top_k=50, top_p=0.90
Implement dynamic token budget per dataset: budget = f(n_rows, n_cols, n_unique_vals)
Try two-phase generation: <think> phase (temp=0.3) → JSON phase (temp=0.05)
Re-evaluate current adapter with these inference fixes BEFORE starting v3 training
If inference fixes bring F1 from 7.5% to 40%+, confirms v3 training will push to 0.80+

claude_unique_insights.loop_probability_proof: If P(continue)=0.95 per item, P(terminate over 50 items) = 0.95^50 ≈ 7.7%. This is why small datasets (few items) work but large ones loop. It's a PROBABILITY problem.
claude_unique_insights.alpha_r_ratio_was_key: v2 used alpha/r = 16/64 = 0.25 (very weak updates). Fix to 64/32 = 2.0. This was likely the #2 most impactful issue after CoT format.
claude_unique_insights.temperature_counterintuitive: temp=0.1 makes loops WORSE (near-deterministic, locks into attractor). temp=0.3 adds enough randomness to escape. F1 degradation from 0.1→0.3 typically <3%.
claude_unique_insights.two_phase_generation: Generate <think> with temp=0.3 (diversity), then switch to temp=0.05 for JSON (precision). Implement with StoppingCriteria at </think>.
claude_unique_insights.evaluation_gate: Explicit pass/fail criteria between SFT and DPO: parse≥0.70, F1≥0.55, loop≤0.30. Do NOT run DPO if SFT is broken.
claude_unique_insights.compact_cot_format: Column-grouped: 'age: young=4(R1,R2,R4,R7)✓ old=3(R3,R5,R8)✓' + FREQUENT SINGLES [N] summaries + RESULT SUMMARY termination signal.
claude_unique_insights.dpo_cannot_fix_broken_sft: DPO heavily penalizes looping tokens, but only works if the SFT base can already sometimes produce correct outputs. Fix SFT first, then DPO.

priority_ranking_by_impact.action: Compact CoT format with RESULT SUMMARY termination
Reduce epochs to 3 with early stopping

claude_unique_insights.key_quote: The v2 adapter at 78.6% F1 on compatible datasets proves the learning is correct. The gap between 78.6% and 7.5% mean F1 is almost entirely explained by the repetition loop on larger datasets. Fix the loop → fix the model.
